data.title: Applied Materials slides on Morgan Stanley downgrade; sees 2025 as 'transition' year
data.description:
Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) was in focus on Thursday as Morgan Stanley downgrade the semiconductor equipment firm, citing the belief 2025 will be a "transition" year for the wafer fab equipment market.

Applied Materials shares fell 2% in premarket trading as it was cut to Underweight from Equal-Weight and the price target was lowered to $179 from $164.

"Following record [wafer fab equipment] in 2024 lead by DRAM and China, we forecast 2025 to be a transition year," analysts at the firm wrote. "The longer-term secular drivers of [wafer fab equipment] market growth are intact as supply chain diversification remains top of mind and semiconductor capital intensity is not declining. However, end-markets matter and so does equipment purchase timing, and with declining DRAM ASP and significant pull-forward of Western equipment by Chinese players, we expect 2025 to be a transition year as companies digest capacity build from the prior year."

Overall, it sees wafer fab equipment spending down 6% year-over-year in 2025, with dynamic random access memory-related spending likely down 10% (despite 20% growth from high-bandwidth memory); NAND-related spending up 22%; spending from China down 15% (a 32% increase for Chinese companies compared to a 24% decline for Western companies); and foundry logic down roughly 6%.

"We forecast combined capex from [Taiwan Semiconductor], Intel, and Samsung to decline 2% [year-over-year] in 2025, but we have long-term questions around the foundry aspirations of Intel and Samsung," the analysts added.

The firm also tweaked its price targets on KLA Corp. (KLAC) and Lam Research (LRCX) to $703 and $78 from $746 and $76, respectively.

data.title: Dollar General in charts: Q3 Same-store sales up 1.3% Y/Y; consumables sales up 6.4% Y/Y
Myomo dips on $15M equity offering
data.description:
• Myomo (NYSE:MYO) has priced an underwritten public offering of 3M common shares at $5.00 per share for gross proceeds of approximately $15M.
  • Underwriter have a 30-day option to purchase up to 450,000 additional shares of its common stock at the public offering price.
  • The net proceeds together with its existing cash and cash equivalents will be used to accelerate its revenue growth by funding an increase in advertising expenses and headcount to support the expected revenue growth in Myomo’s direct billing channel, along with investments in systems to support growth in the O&P channel and in scaling its operations. In addition, the net proceeds of the offering will be used to fund product development and sustaining engineering activities to enhance the current MyoPro product line and to develop the next generation of Myomo’s products, with the remainder for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • MYO -7.8% premarket to $4.93
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CME declares annual variable dividend of $5.80, announces $3B stock buyback
data.description:
• CME (NASDAQ:CME) declares $5.80/share annual variable dividend, up from $5.25 variable annual dividend declared last year.
  • Payable Jan. 16; for shareholders of record Dec. 27; ex-div Dec. 27.
  • The Board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $3 billion of CME Group Class A common stock.
  • See CME Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Upbound Group raises dividend by 5.4% to $0.39
data.description:
• Upbound Group (NASDAQ:UPBD) declares $0.39/share quarterly dividend, 5.4% increase from prior dividend of $0.37.
  • Forward yield 4.58%
  • Payable Jan. 7; for shareholders of record Dec. 18; ex-div Dec. 18.
  • See UPBD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: U.S. employers' announced job cuts rise 3.8% in November, climb 27% Y/Y: Challenger Report
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 57,727 job cuts in November, up 3.8% from those announced in October and up 27% Y/Y, the outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas said Thursday.

Year-to-date, companies announced 722,566 job cuts, a 5.2% increase from those announced in the first 11 months of 2023. Excluding the year-to-date total of 2.23M in 2020, announced job cuts are the highest they've been since 2009.

"November saw downstream cuts to Automotive suppliers and parts manufacturers, as well as ongoing cuts in Consumer and Industrial Manufacturing," said Andrew Challenger, senior vice president of Challenger, Gray & Christmas, Inc. "Technology also saw a high number of layoff announcements, as that sector continues to undergo significant changes."

The automotive sector announced 11,506 job cuts in November, the most of any sector and the highest monthly total since April. Year-to-date, automotive companies announced 45,820 job cuts, up 59% from the first 11 months of last year.

"The automotive industry is currently experiencing significant challenges, including potential tariffs affecting U.S. automakers with overseas factories, intensifying competition from Chinese electric vehicle ("EV") manufacturers, and shifts in government subsidies for EVs," Challenger said.

Technology companies reported 10,231 job cuts for a total of 130,791 year-to-date, down 2% from the same period a year ago.

The healthcare and healthcare products industry, excluding hospitals, announced 47,249 job cuts this year, down 18% from the 11-month-period in 2023.

Industrial manufacturing also saw weakness, announcing 21,974 job cuts through November, up 158% from the same period last year.

U.S.-based employers announced 761,954 hiring plans for the first 11 months of the year, down 2% the same period a year ago, and the lowest level since 2015.

data.title: ICL President and CEO Raviv Zoller plans to depart in early 2025, successor search begins
data.description:
• ICL (NYSE:ICL) president and CEO, Raviv Zoller is expected to leave the company in early 2025, following both the appointment of a replacement and a formal transition.
  • CEO Zoller will continue to serve the company until a replacement has been announced.
  • ICL -3.1% premarket to $4.85
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SolarEdge Technologies taps current CMO Nir as new CEO
data.description:
SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) said Thursday it appointed Chief Marketing Officer Shuki Nir as its new CEO effective immediately, succeeding Ronen Faier, who has served as interim CEO since August.

Nir has served as SolarEdge's (SEDG) CMO since June; previously he held several key roles including General Manager of the consumer business unit at SanDisk and also led SanDisk's corporate marketing.

Faier, who became CEO when Zvi Lando stepped down, joined the company in 2011 as its CFO and was general manager of the SolarEdge Storage Division during 2019-23.

data.title: Cango shares rise on November bitoin production update
data.description:
• Cango (NYSE:CANG) has completed the acquisition of on-rack crypto mining machines with an aggregate hashrate of 32 Exahash per second ("EH") for a total purchase price of US$256 million in cash.
  • The company has produced 363.9 bitcoins in November, resulting in 18.85 bitcoins produced each day. 
  • Total number of Bitcoins held as of November 30, 2024: 363.9
  • The company has deployed hashrate of 32 EH/, with average operating hashrate: 29.75 EH/s
  • CANG +3.6% premarket to $4.22
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bitdeer Technologies mines 150 bitcoins in November, down 63% Y/Y
data.description:
• Blockchain technology company Bitdeer Technologies Group (NASDAQ:BTDR) mined 150 bitcoins in November, down 13.79% from 174 bitcoins in October and 62.78% from 403 in November 2023.
  • BTDR shares were trading +10.88% Thursday pre-market at $17.83.
  • Hash rate under management came in at 20.7 EH/s, compared to 18.0 EH/s a month ago and 21.1 EH/s a year ago.
  • Bitdeer Technologies reported November holdings of 443 bitcoins.
  • Source: Press Release
